User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account on the PlatformStream blog platform, managing authentication and ownership of content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The unique email address of the user, used for login and identification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Blog.title: Blog
Blog.description: Represents a blog post created by a user, including content, publication status, and metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Blog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who authored this blog post. (Relationship: User 1:N Blog)

title.description: The title of the blog post.

slug.description: A unique, URL-friendly identifier for the blog post.

content.description: The main body content of the blog post.

summary.description: An AI-generated concise summary of the blog post content, used for previews.

isPublished.description: A flag indicating whether the blog post is publicly accessible.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the blog post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the blog post was last updated.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a user's 'like' on a specific blog post, ensuring a user can like a post only once.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Like entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who liked the blog post. (Relationship: User 1:N Like)

blogId.description: Reference to the Blog post that was liked. (Relationship: Blog 1:N Like)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the like was recorded.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a user's comment on a specific blog post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

blogId.description: Reference to the Blog post on which the comment was made. (Relationship: Blog 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who posted the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the comment was posted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Each document is uniquely identified by the user's authentication UID. Data is private to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores blog posts that are in a draft (unpublished) state, accessible only by the author. Uses path-based authorization. Implicitly 'isPublished: false'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this draft blog.

params.name: blogId
params.description: The unique ID of the blog post (draft).

definition.description: Stores blog posts that are publicly accessible. All documents in this collection are implicitly 'isPublished: true'. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: blogId
params.description: The unique ID of the blog post (published).

definition.description: Records a user's 'like' on a specific published blog post. Includes denormalized 'blogOwnerId' for authorization independence and 'userId' for liker ownership. Implicitly associated with a published blog.

params.name: blogId
params.description: The unique ID of the published blog post that was liked.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique ID of the like document, typically a user's ID to ensure one like per user per blog.

definition.description: Stores comments made on a specific published blog post. Includes denormalized 'blogOwnerId' for authorization independence and 'userId' for commenter ownership. Implicitly associated with a published blog.

params.name: blogId
params.description: The unique ID of the published blog post on which the comment was made.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the comment.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for PlatformStream prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Accurate Projections (QAPs) by employing strong structural segregation and strategic denormalization. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Ownership:** All user-specific private data, such as user profiles and draft blogs, are directly nested under `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership ensures that authorization rules can directly leverage `request.auth.uid` against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to verify ownership.
2.  **Blog Ownership (Published):** For published blogs in `/published_blogs/{blogId}`, each document explicitly includes an `ownerId` field. This denormalization allows rules to check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` for write operations without querying the original user or draft blog document. This upholds the critical mandate of avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies.
3.  **Comment/Like Ownership:** Similarly, `Comment` and `Like` documents, nested under `/published_blogs/{blogId}`, include `userId` (the author/liker) for self-management and `blogOwnerId` (the owner of the parent blog) for potential blog owner moderation rights. This ensures all necessary authorization context is available within the document itself, preventing `get()` calls to the parent blog or user documents.

**QAPs (Query-Accurate Projections):**
1.  **Structural Segregation for Blogs:** The most significant design choice for QAPs is the segregation of blog posts into two distinct top-level collections: `/users/{userId}/draft_blogs` and `/published_blogs`. 
    *   `/users/{userId}/draft_blogs`: This collection exclusively holds blogs that are in a draft state (`isPublished: false`). Access is strictly limited to the owning user. Listing operations here naturally return only private drafts, satisfying owner-specific queries.
    *   `/published_blogs`: This collection exclusively holds blogs that are publicly accessible (`isPublished: true`). The "Public Feed" (paginated list of published blogs) can perform a direct `list` query on this collection. Since all documents in this collection are guaranteed to be published, no additional `isPublished == true` filter is required in the Firestore security rules, making the `list` operation efficient and robust against rule-filtering limitations. Public blog access by `slug` also queries this collection directly, ensuring only published content is returned.
2.  **Comments and Likes:** `Comments` and `Likes` are modeled as subcollections directly under `/published_blogs/{blogId}`. This structure inherently ensures that comments and likes only exist for published blogs. When requesting `/blogs/:id/comments` or `/blogs/:id/likes`, queries are performed on these subcollections, which automatically fulfill the requirement that these interactions are only available on published content, without needing `get()` calls to check the parent blog's status.

This design leverages the core principles to create a secure, scalable, and highly debuggable Firestore schema, where authorization intent is clear, and security rules are simplified due to the structure and denormalized context.
